name: Azure Subscription Permission Elevation Via ActivityLogs
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Azure Platform
when the event matches Operation Name (custom) is any of MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ELEVATEACCESS/ACTION
TD_list: APPLY Azure Subscription Permission Elevation Via ActivityLogs on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Azure Platform
AND when the event matches Operation Name (custom) is any of MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ELEVATEACCESS/ACTION
RED_list: Event name: Azure Subscription Permission Elevation Via ActivityLogs

Event description: Detects when a user has been elevated to manage all Azure Subscriptions.
    This change should be investigated immediately if it isn't planned.
    This setting could allow an attacker access to Azure subscriptions in your environment.

falsepositives:- If this was approved by System Administrator.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
Include detected events by "Username" from this point forward, in the offense, for 900 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
